_meta.description: Annotations — podcast summaries, earnings call highlights, analyst notes, and research memos. Keyed to vertical and/or ticker. Add entries chronologically.
_meta.instructions: Each entry has: id (slug), type, date (YYYY-MM-DD), vertical (optional), ticker (optional), title, body, tags[].

annotations.title: China REE weaponization escalates to full export licensing — April 2025 controls still active
annotations.body: April 2025 was the inflection point. China placed seven heavy/medium rare earth elements under export licensing: samarium, gadolinium, terbium, dysprosium, lutetium, scandium, and yttrium — plus their metals, oxides, alloys, and magnet materials. The 'pause' narrative in late 2025 was a misread: only the second tranche (five additional elements) was deferred to November 2026. The April controls were never suspended. Critically, an extraterritorial '50% rule' applies: products made outside China using Chinese-origin REEs also require export licenses — unprecedented jurisdictional reach. Export volumes fell sharply April-May 2025, forcing carmakers and defense suppliers to cut utilization or seek alternatives. MP Materials responded by signing a government price protection agreement (DoD floor at $110/kg NdPr) and ending all China sales. Lynas recorded H1 2026 net income A$80.21M (+1,259% YoY), delivered first heavy REE (Dy/Tb) sales, and now targets A$1.1B revenue in 2026. The geopolitical regime has permanently shifted: REE is no longer a risk to monitor — it is an active supply disruption requiring defensive positioning.

annotations.title: Nuclear AI PPA buildout accelerates far beyond CEG-Microsoft template
annotations.body: The CEG-Microsoft Three Mile Island PPA was the template, not the ceiling. As of May 2026, the hyperscaler nuclear PPA pipeline has expanded dramatically: Amazon/AWS signed a 17-year PPA with Talen Energy for 1.92 GW from the Susquehanna nuclear plant (June 2025); Google signed with Kairos Power for a fleet of SMRs targeting 500 MW by 2030 (first corporate SMR PPA ever); Meta announced a 6.6 GW nuclear procurement strategy for its 'Prometheus' AI data center project. Nuclear is winning the 24/7 carbon-free baseload competition for AI infrastructure because it uniquely combines price certainty (15-20yr contracts at $110-130/MWh), zero-carbon credentials, and 90%+ capacity factor. Uranium market: spot ~$86/lb, LT contract price $93/lb (highest since 2008). Cameco McArthur River experienced a flooding event May 10, 2026, with production restored by May 27 — a 17-day disruption with no lasting impact on 2026 guidance (19.5-21.5M lbs maintained). Centrus Energy: DOE exercised Phase III HALEU production option at Piketon (~$110M through June 2026) with 8-year extension options. $2.3B contingent backlog. Scale challenge: Centrus targets 12 metric tons/year HALEU capacity 'sometime after 2030' — advanced reactor developers need this fuel but commercial-scale supply is years away.

annotations.title: Gold ATH $5,589/oz — streaming companies generating exceptional FCF; RGLD transforms via Sandstorm
annotations.body: Gold hit $5,589/oz on January 28, 2026 — highest ever recorded. As of late May 2026, gold is ~$4,523/oz but central bank buying remains structurally above pre-2022 averages: Q1 2026 net central bank purchases 244 tonnes; Goldman projects ~60 tonnes/month through year-end; J.P. Morgan projects $5,000/oz by Q4 2026. The de-dollarization thesis is playing out in the data. At these prices, the streaming companies are generating exceptional FCF. Franco-Nevada (FNV): 2025 record revenue $1,822.8M (+YoY); net income $1.11B (2x YoY); operating cash flow $1,494M; dividend raised 16%. Crucially, FNV built this record entirely WITHOUT any Cobre Panama contribution — any restart is pure free optionality. Wheaton Precious Metals (WPM): Has overtaken FNV as world's largest precious metals streamer by market cap. 2026 GEO guidance 860K-940K (record); expanded Antamina silver stream adds directly. Royal Gold (RGLD): Acquired Sandstorm Gold ($3.5B deal, closed October 2025) plus $1.0B Kansanshi stream — $5.4B of M&A in 2025 transforms RGLD from mid-tier royalty to large-cap streaming house. 25th consecutive annual dividend increase. The streaming structure is most compelling at peak gold prices: fixed-cost streams + full commodity upside = maximum FCF leverage with no SA operational risk.

annotations.title: Lithium cycle turn confirmed — trough behind us; ALB +148% EBITDA; SQM Atacama rights to 2060
annotations.body: The lithium winter is over. Li carbonate (LCE) troughed at approximately $8,000/tonne mid-2025 (down ~83% from the 2022 peak) and has recovered to approximately $21,000/tonne by May 2026 — a 162% rebound from trough. Two earnings prints confirm the turn. Albemarle (ALB) Q1 2026: net sales +33% to $1.43B; adjusted EBITDA +148% YoY to $664M; adjusted EPS $2.95 vs. $1.19 consensus — a massive beat. Energy Storage segment revenue +70% to $891M. Realized price $17/kg LCE in Q1, guiding higher through the year. ALB also cut $1.3B of debt — balance sheet repair ahead of schedule. SQM Q1 2026: ~69,000 MT LCE shipped at full capacity; volume guidance upgraded from +10% to +15% growth for 2026; SQM forecasts 25% global lithium demand growth for 2026. Most importantly: the Codelco joint venture has resolved SQM's single largest overhang — Atacama operating rights now secured to 2060 (removing the nationalization/concession-expiry risk that had weighed on the stock for 3 years). Structural demand thesis unchanged: 2026-2030 EV penetration >30% globally + grid-scale BESS scaling independently (BESS economics now self-sustaining without IRA credits). The trough is behind us but this vertical is still inning 4 — cycle-sensitive and requires continued timing discipline.

annotations.title: Iran strikes Qatar Ras Laffan — US LNG becomes geopolitically irreplaceable for Europe
annotations.body: March 18, 2026: Iran struck Qatar's Ras Laffan LNG complex — the world's largest LNG facility. Approximately 17% of Qatar's LNG production capacity damaged. Repair timeline estimated at 3-5 years. European gas benchmark futures jumped 35% on the day. Asian LNG spot prices spiked 140%+. This event structurally reinforces the US LNG thesis in a way that was not in any base case. EU gas storage: 46 BCM entering 2026 (vs. 60 BCM in 2025, 77 BCM in 2024); as of late May 2026, EU storage at approximately 35% vs. 48% five-year average — critically low heading into the 2026-27 winter. US LNG response: record exports of 11.7M tonnes in March 2026 with approximately 64% directed to Europe. Cheniere (LNG): upgraded across Wall Street post-conflict. 2025 record results: $19.976B revenue (+27% YoY), net income $5.33B (+64%), >46M tonnes delivered. Corpus Christi Stage 3 Train 5 achieved first LNG in February 2026; Trains 6-7 on track for 2026 completion; >95% of capacity contracted through 2030. New 1.2 mtpa deal with CPC Corporation (Taiwan) through 2050. The Qatar disruption makes the 20-year European LNG SPAs not just commercially important but existentially necessary — US LNG is now geopolitically irreplaceable for European energy security.

annotations.title: US critical minerals policy acceleration — Project Vault, One Big Beautiful Bill, EXIM loans
annotations.body: The US policy response to Chinese critical mineral weaponization has substantially upgraded in 2025-2026. Key actions: (1) One Big Beautiful Bill Act (signed July 4, 2025): $2B to expand National Defense Stockpile, $5B to Industrial Base Fund for critical mineral supply chains, $500M to DOD Credit Program, $1B DPA financing through September 2027. (2) Project Vault (February 2026): $12B public-private critical minerals reserve — largest EXIM Bank loan in its 92-year history, plus direct equity stakes in mining companies and $7.5B congressional appropriations. (3) January 15, 2026 Executive Order: ties US critical minerals security to global partnerships; preferential trading zone framework open to 50+ allied nations with enforceable fair market prices. Key beneficiaries in portfolio: MP Materials (government floor price $110/kg NdPr), Perpetua Resources ($2.7B EXIM loan for Stibnite), Centrus Energy (HALEU contract Phase III), Materion (defense-prime $65M beryllium capacity investment). Context: US is fully import-dependent on 12 critical minerals and relies on imports for >50% of consumption of 29 additional minerals. China leads production of 30 of 60 minerals on the 2025 critical minerals list. The policy is real — the capital is starting to flow — but the structural dependency is decades in the making.

annotations.title: Q1 2026 earnings confirm infrastructure supercycle: ETN 228 GW DC backlog, GEV +71% orders, PWR $48.5B
annotations.body: The Q1 2026 earnings season put hard numbers on the infrastructure supercycle. Eaton (ETN): revenue $7.5B (+17% YoY), organic growth 10%; total data center backlog now 228 GW — equivalent to 12 years of orders at current build rates; Electrical Americas orders +42% YoY; data center orders +240% YoY. Closed $11B of acquisitions in Q1 2026 including Boyd Thermal (DC cooling) and Ultra PCS Limited (aerospace power). GE Vernova (GEV): Q1 2026 orders $18.3B (+71% organically). Signed 21 GW of new gas equipment contracts in Q1 alone. Gas turbine backlog + slot reservations target raised to 110 GW. Overall backlog growing from $116B at spinoff to $163B; $200B target pulled forward from 2028 to 2027. Electrification (Grid) segment orders +86% organically. 2026 guidance: $44-45B revenue, $5.0-5.5B free cash flow. Quanta Services (PWR): Q1 2026 revenue $7.87B (+26% YoY). Backlog record $48.5B — up from approximately $28B two years ago (+73% in two years). 2026 full-year guidance raised above consensus. Key observation: these are not forecast numbers — they are executed contracts with creditworthy counterparties. 228 GW, $48.5B, $18.3B orders — the supercycle is in the earnings data, not just the narrative.

annotations.title: Perpetua Resources: Stibnite construction commenced, $2.7B EXIM loan, DoD funding — no longer a junior miner
annotations.body: October 21, 2025: early works construction commenced at the Stibnite Gold Project following Forest Service Final Record of Decision. This is the binary catalyst that was the basis for the conviction position — it has now fired. Financing: US EXIM Board approved $2.7B senior secured loan (includes ~$2.2B direct loan). Over $850M equity raised. $714M cash on hand at year-end 2025. DoD awards: >$80M combined for antimony research, construction readiness, permitting, and engineering. Revised capex estimate: $2.576B. Economics: base-case NPV5%: $3.5B at $3,250/oz gold; $6.1B at $4,500/oz gold. With gold currently ~$4,500/oz, the NPV sensitivity case is effectively the base case. Final investment decision expected spring 2026. Antimony context: China's September 2024 export licensing sent antimony from ~$12,000/tonne to a peak of ~$57,778/tonne by April 2025 (380% spike), now holding ~$51,800/tonne — permanently repriced. Stibnite will be the only significant US domestic antimony source. The narrative has completely shifted: Perpetua is no longer a permitting-stage junior miner — it is a construction-stage, DoD-backed, EXIM-financed strategic national asset with economics that improve with every dollar of gold price.

annotations.title: Valero Q1 2026: complete earnings reversal — $1.3B profit vs. $595M loss in Q1 2025
annotations.body: Valero Q1 2026 net income: $1.3B ($4.22/share) vs. a net loss of $595M in Q1 2025 — an extraordinary swing. Refining operating income: $1.81B; refining margin: $14.90/bbl; US Gulf Coast distillate margin: $27.60/bbl. Renewable diesel segment: operating income $139M in Q1 2026 vs. operating loss of $141M in Q1 2025 — a $280M swing driven by tight distillate inventories and the Port Arthur sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) project successfully commissioning. The renewable diesel drag that weighed on VLO through 2024-25 has reversed to profitability. St. Charles FCC optimization on track for Q3 2026. The refining margin expansion magnitude is striking. Key variable to watch: crack spread cycles — Gulf Coast 321 crack spread is the primary earnings driver. The structural case for VLO has always been post-2020 refinery closure-driven structural tightening, which appears to be reasserting.

annotations.title: Robotics REE demand multiplier — ~28 magnet kits per humanoid, JL Mag pure-play thesis
annotations.body: The robotics demand layer is the most underpriced REE catalyst in the market. BOM teardown of Tesla Optimus: approximately 28 rare-earth magnet assemblies per robot (frameless torque motors in every joint). At ~$20 of magnet content per humanoid, the per-unit dollar is modest — but the volume leverage is compounding on top of the EV and wind turbine demand that already exists. Citrini's framing: 'Every joint motor needs rare-earth magnets' — robotics scaling is not a substitution for EV demand, it is additive. Pure-play names on the magnet bottleneck: MP Materials (MP — in portfolio, commercial magnet ramp H2 2026); JL Mag (6880.HK — China's dominant sintered NdFeB maker, captures the volume regardless of Western supply ambitions); Lynas (LYC.AU — upstream separation feeding magnet supply chains). Contrarian signal of note: Allient (in AI hardware map) is actively redesigning motors to reduce or eliminate rare-earth content — this motor-redesign behavior historically precedes scarcity rent because OEMs only do it when procurement teams are alarmed about supply. The demand multiplier from robotics is not yet in most sell-side REE demand models.

annotations.title: SiC supply chain inheritance — EV-built capacity now being claimed by AI 800V infrastructure
annotations.body: The largest hard-assets-relevant macro theme from the AI hardware supply chain: SiC demand was built from a $300M industrial niche (2018) to $3.5B (2024) entirely on the back of EV drivetrain demand. Now, as European EV adoption softens, AI infrastructure is inheriting that capacity — and will accelerate past it. 800V DC rack architecture (Rubin generation, first meaningful AI deployment 2026, steep 2028-2030 ramp) requires SiC MOSFETs for power conversion; AI is projected to represent approximately half of total SiC demand by 2030. Wolfspeed (WOLF): world's only commercial 200mm SiC wafer fab; demonstrated the first 300mm SiC crystal in January 2026 — only Coherent has shown this at the research stage. Post-Chapter 11 filing (2025), WOLF is mispriced against the old EV investor cohort that has abandoned SiC exposure while AI demand is accelerating. ROHM (6963.JP): Toyota BZ5 production win with 4th-gen SiC MOSFETs — Japan's leading SiC device maker with both EV and AI server power supply exposure. Speculative next layer: SiC has ~3× copper's thermal conductivity → next architecture move is SiC-based cold plates and fluid-cooled SiC interposers directly under the AI die (Eotron/NASA private work) — this would pull SiC from the rack periphery into the package itself, a material-pricing-power flip. Key principle: 'The closer a material sits to the chip, the more pricing leverage its vendor has over the customer.'

annotations.title: AI materials PCB/substrate basket — HVLP copper foil, T-glass, CCL, ABF near-monopoly
annotations.body: The AI PCB/substrate material layer is a hard-assets bottleneck hiding inside the semiconductor supply chain. Four acute chokepoints (Citrini Tier-1): (1) HVLP copper foil (high-voltage, low-profile) for AI server PCBs: 'few players capable of production'; capacity relief pushed from this year to 2027+. Pure-plays: Mitsui Kinzoku (5706.JP) and Co-Tech (8358.TW). (2) T-glass (high-frequency glass cloth reinforcement for AI PCBs): capacity relief similarly pushed to 2027+. Mostly Japanese specialty glass producers. (3) CCL (copper-clad laminate): Korean CCL prices +74.5% YoY as of mid-2026; lead times stretched. Pure-plays: Elite Material (2383.TW) and ITEQ (6213.TW). (4) ABF film (Ajinomoto Build-up Film): near-monopoly held by Ajinomoto (2802.T) — already in the AI hardware map at conviction-3. The single-name with broadest multi-bottleneck exposure: Resonac (4004.JP, ex-Showa Denko) — specialty materials across SiC, abrasives, HAST test films, and AI substrate materials. Key investment principle applies across this basket: these materials sit between the PCB fabricator and the chip package — as AI PCB specs tighten with each GPU generation, fewer suppliers can meet qualification, and those who can gain lasting pricing power.

annotations.title: Solid-state transformer race — SiC-based SSTs vs. iron-core transformers; 2027 deployment window
annotations.body: Iron-core transformer lead times of 18-30 months have become the binding constraint on hyperscaler data center buildout — projects are now bottlenecked on transformer delivery rather than land, power access, or chip supply. This has opened a deployment window for solid-state transformers (SSTs): SiC-based switching architecture, lead times of months not years, no iron core, no insulating oil, and software-controllable power conversion. The race to production-ready SST: Heron Power — closed $140M Series B February 2026 (a16z American Dynamism + Breakthrough Energy Ventures; Drew Baglino, ex-Tesla SVP Powertrain, as CEO). Claims 50 GW of letters of intent; plans 40 GW/yr manufacturing facility. DG Matrix — $60M Series A, same window, with ABB and Mitsubishi Heavy participating as strategic investors. Eaton acquired Resilient Power (up to $150M) — the clearest signal from an incumbent transformer maker that SSTs are a credible replacement path. Scale application: NY MTA needs 190× 4MW power-traction substations for subway electrification — this is the municipal infrastructure use case alongside hyperscaler data centers. If SSTs reach production scale by 2027, they relieve the transformer bottleneck but simultaneously create a new SiC substrate demand pulse — reinforcing the Wolfspeed/ROHM/COHR thesis. Listed incumbents (ETN, GEV, ABB, Siemens Hitachi Energy) are all watching SST closely; the question is whether the private SST pure-plays IPO before the window closes.

annotations.title: System initialization — hard assets research framework
annotations.body: Initial build of the hard assets research system. 12 verticals mapped across 10 value chain stages. Three structural clusters defined: Early Innings (REE inning 1, Nuclear/Uranium inning 2, Electricity/Grid inning 2, Copper inning 2, Tech Metals inning 2, Water/Land inning 2, NG/LNG inning 3), Mid Innings (Li/Battery inning 4, Fertilizers inning 4, Ind. Inputs inning 3, PGMs/Gold/Ag inning 4), and Cycle-Sensitive (Oil inning 5). Defining insight: Nuclear, REE, and Electricity simultaneously at inning 1-2 with highest capex gaps (90, 92, 88) and lowest business cycle risk (<30) — most defensible structural positions in a recessionary environment.
